properties.name: CTOE - Centro de Tropas de Operações Especiais (Special Operations Troops Centre)
properties.description: The CTOE contains an operational unit called DOE, its Special Operations Detachment, popularly known as Rangers, tasked with performing missions similar to the US Army's Delta Force or British Special Air Service. Some of these missions include conducting Long-Range Reconnaissance Patrols (LRRP), raids against high-value targets, locating enemy command and control centres, targeting and destruction of enemy air defences and radar systems, and POW rescue operations. The unit can be infiltrated by parachute, helicopter, small boat, or by foot.

properties.name: Campo de Tiro de Alcochete (Field Firing Range of Alcochete)
properties.description:
The Field Firing Range of Alcochete (Portuguese: Campo de Tiro de Alcochete, CTA) is a military firing and bombing range. As part of its activities, the range has a permanent bomb disposal unit and its security is handled by the Polícia Aérea.

The field firing range is currently used by all branches of the Portuguese Armed Forces for military maneuvers involving units preparing for deployment, evaluation of new equipment, large scale military exercises and Explosive Ordnance Disposal (EOD) training.

In addition to the Air Force units undergoing training and live ammunition exercises, the field firing range is also used by 552 Squadron for helicopter gun-mounted machine gun and cannon live fire training; 201 and 301 Squadron also use it for both air-to-air and air-to-ground training. This involves the firing of Mk-82, Mk-84, GBU-12 Paveway, GBU-49, GBU-31, GBU-33 and Mk-106 ordnance. In addition, the range has an all-terrain track certified by the National Firefighter School (Portuguese: Escola Nacional de Bombeiros) which is used for training by the Air Force's rescue and firefighting units.

The Portuguese Army makes further use of the complex for mortar and artillery training and MILAN anti-tank missile instruction courses. Airborne units make use of the area for parachute jumps due to the field's proximity to the Montijo Air Base, where the Air Force's C-295 and C-130 transport aircraft are based.

properties.name: Base Naval de Lisboa (Lisbon Naval Base)
properties.description:
Base Naval de Lisboa (Lisbon Naval Base), is the main installation and operational base of the Portuguese Navy.
Most of the Portuguese Naval fleet ships are based there, as well as many of its administrative, training and support services. Within its perimeter are also located the Naval School, the Naval Technologies School, the Alfeite Arsenal, the Marine corps Base and other Portuguese Navy units.

(Monte Real Air Base)
properties.description:
Monte Real Air Base, officially designated as Air Force Base No. 5 (Portuguese: Base Aérea Nº 5, BA5), is a Portuguese Air Force Military airbase.

Monte Real Air Base hosts the 51st Operational Group (GO51). The GO51's operational fighter squadrons are:

201 Fighter Squadron "Falcões" (Falcons)
- F-16 Fighting Falcon M (2010—Present)
301 Fighter Squadron "Jaguares" (Jaguars)
- F-16 Fighting Falcon AM/BM (2005—present)

103 Sqn. "Caracóis" (Snails) — complementary flying training and operational transition training.
- Dassault/Dornier Alpha Jet (1993—present)
552 Sqn. "Zangões" (Drones) — tactical air transport operations and complementary flying training in helicopters.
- Sud Aviation SE.3160 Alouette III
601 Sqn. "Lobos" (Wolves) — maritime patrol squadron.
- Lockheed P-3C CUP+ Orion

properties.name: Base Aérea das Lajes (Lajes Field)
properties.description:
Lajes Field (Portuguese: Base Aérea das Lajes), officially designated Air Base No. 4 (Base Aérea Nº 4, BA4) (IATA: TER, ICAO: LPLA), is a multi-use airfield, home to the Portuguese Air Force Base Aérea N º4 and Azores Air Zone Command (Portuguese: Comando da Zona Aérea dos Açores), a United States Air Force detachment unit (operated by the 65th Air Base Group of United States Air Forces in Europe - Air Forces Africa), and a regional air passenger terminal located near Lajes.

Portuguese Air Force / Azores Aerial Detachment:
- Esq. 502 "Elefantes" - tactical and general transport duties, medical evacuation, maritime patrol, and search and rescue support; flying one EADS CASA C-295, deployed from Montijo AB.
- Esq. 751 "Pumas" - search and rescue squadron flying two AgustaWestland AW101, deployed from Montijo AB.

United States Forces Azores:
Lajes Field is the home of the 65th Air Base Group, which in turn is subordinate to the United States Air Forces in Europe. The group provides base and en route support for the U.S. Department of Defense, NATO, and other authorized aircraft transiting the installation.

In addition to the 65th Air Base Wing, other units at Lajes Field include the U.S. Army Military Traffic Management Command’s 1324th Military Port Command in the nearby port of Praia da Vitoria, U.S. Air Force Air Mobility Command’s 729th Air Mobility Support Squadron, Detachment 6 of the Air Force Public Affairs Agency, Detachment 250 - Air Force Office of Special Investigations, Defense Property Disposal Office, and the Defense Commissary Agency.

Lajes Field is also the home of the 65th Communication Squadron, which provides communication in the form of High Frequency Global Communications Systems (HFGCS), ground radio, ground radar, SatCom (Satellite Communications), and cryptography to the base.

properties.name: Vilkovo 2

properties.description: The last remaining Kashin class destroyer Smetlivy, was modernised in 1990 and remains in service with the Black Sea Fleet

properties.name: SSV-33 Ural
properties.description: SSV-33 Ural, originally the hull of a Kirov class, SSV-33 was modified for electronic warfare. Originally envisioned to spy on US Nuclear and Ballisitc Missile programs, the ship was plagued with mishaps. She was not considered viable and decomissioned. Seen here pending removal of her nuclear reactor after having her superstructure removed at Bolshoy Kamen in 2012.

properties.name: Typhoon Class, Dimitriy Donskoy
properties.description: Only active Typhoon Class, used for missile testing

properties.name: TK-17, TK-20; Retired
properties.description: Retired Typhoon classes Arkhangelsk and Severstal.

properties.name: Slava Class, Ukrayina
properties.description: 4th ship of the Slava Class, unfinished and remains moored at Mykolaiv. Some discussion has taken place over the purchase of her by Russia.

properties.name: Slava Class, Moskva
properties.description: 1st ship of the Slava class, Moskva (ex Slava)

properties.name: Slava Class, Marshal Ustinov
properties.description: Slava Class Marshal Ustinov of the northern fleet, in refit at the time. She has now returned to service

properties.name: Kirov Class, Admiral Nakhimov
properties.description: In refit, scheduled to return to service between 2018  and 2020.

properties.name: Kirov Class, Admiral Lazarev
properties.description: Kirov Class, Admiral Lazarev (ex Frunze). Laid up awaiting possible refit. Refit is unlikely as she and Admiral Ushakov have serious reactor issues, though Lazarev has been repainted to her original condition. Were she to enter refit it would likely not be until the late 2030; after both Admiral Nakhimov and subsequently Peter the Great leave refit at Severodvinsk.

properties.name: Kirov Class, Admiral Ushakov
properties.description: Kirov Class, Admiral Ushakov (ex Kirov) Laid up following reactor accident. Plans to return all 4 Kirov classes to serive exist, however the reactor issues with her and Admiral Lazaraev make this unlikely.

properties.description: Former RAE Aberporth, operated by QinetiQ on behalf of the MOD as a test & evaluation range.

properties.name: RAF Barkston Heath
properties.description:
To provide tri-service flight training from 2018 onwards using the Grob Perfect T1.

A relief landing site for flights operating out of RAF Cranwell.

properties.name: RAF Barnham
properties.description: Non flying station working as part of RAF Honington. Expected to close by 2020.

properties.name: RRF Benbecula/MOD Hebrides
properties.description: Also known as MOD Hebrides, Remote Radar Head forming part of the UK Air Surveillance and Control System which is managed from RAF Boulmer. The station was formerly known as RAF Benbecula.

properties.name: RAF Benson
properties.description:
Home of the RAF's Puma Support Helicopter Force fleet operating under Joint Helicopter Command.
Super Puma HC2

properties.name: MOD Boscombe Down
properties.description:
Primary Site of Qinetic, operating test and evaluation.Flying Hawk and Alpha Jet. Designated as a QRF station though not being used for this role, Boscombe Down also houses the Southampton University Air Squadron.
Used by Boeing for Chinook maintenance and expected to support Apache and P8 Posiedon in the future.

properties.name: RAF Boulmer
properties.description:
Critical Non-flying station, home to the UK Air Surveillance and Control Systems (UKASCS) Force Command, RAF School of Aerospace Battle Management and a NATO Control and Reporting Centre responsible for monitoring UK airspace.
Supported by a broad network of nationwide RAF facilities.

properties.name: RAF Brize Norton
properties.description:
Homme of the entire RAF logistical fleet, following the transfer of C130 aircraft from RAF Lyneham.
Operating:
C17
C130
A400M
Airbus A330 Voyager

properties.name: RRH Brizlee Wood
properties.description: Remote Radar Head forming part of the UK Air Surveillance and Control System which is managed from RAF Boulmer. The station was formerly known as RAF Brizlee Wood.

properties.name: RRH Buchan
properties.description: Remote Radar Head forming part of the UK Air Surveillance and Control System which is managed from RAF Boulmer The station was formerly known as RAF Buchan.

properties.name: RAF Coningsby
properties.description: QRA Station for Southern England, operating Eurofighter Typhoon front line aircraft and the operational conversion unit. No. 41 also operates older RAF aircraft for test and evaluation.

properties.name: RAF Cosford
properties.description:
A number of training units are based at RAF Cosford, notablyy the University of Birmingham Air Squadron and No. 633 Volunteer Gliding Squadron.

Non flying units include the Defence School of Aeronautical Engineering, No. 1 School of Technical Training, the RAF Aerosystems Engineer and Management Training School, No. 1 Radio School and the RAF School of Physical Training.

properties.name: RAF Cranwell
RAF Digby
properties.description: Non-flying station providing specialist communications support under command of Defence Intelligence. Station personnel are drawn from all three UK armed forces and the US military. Home to the Joint Services Signals Organisation Headquarters, Joint Signals Service Unit, No. 591 Signals Unit and the Aerial Erector School.

properties.name: RAF Flyingdales
properties.description: Critical non-flying station of the RAF, monitors ballistic missile launches and space surveillance capabilities for the UK and US governments.

properties.name: RAF Halton
properties.description:
Training establishment comprising the Recruit Training Squadron, Airmens Command Squadron, International Defence Training, Supply and Management Training Wing, Specialist Training Squadron and several other RAF and joint support units.
A grass airfield provides a base for No. 613 Volunteer Gliding Squadron and several RAF flying clubs.

Expected to close by 2022.

properties.name: RAF Henlow
properties.description:
Support station, home to several RAF and Joint Forces Command units including the RAF Centre of Aviation Medicine, the Joint Arms Control Implementation Group (JACIG), elements of Engineering Operations under Information Systems & Services (ISS), Defence Cultural Specialist Unit, the Band of the Queen's Division and specialist units of the RAF Police.
A grass airfield provides a base for No. 616 Volunteer Gliding Squadron and two flying clubs.

Another station that is expected to close by 2022.

properties.name: RAF High Wycombe
properties.description: Non-flying administrative support station, home to Headquarters RAF Air Command, No. 1 Group, No. 2 Group, No. 22 Group and No. 38 Group.

properties.name: RAF Kinloss
properties.description: Former RAF Kinloss is now manned by a skeleton staff of RAF crew, used as a relief landing ground for RAF Lossiemouth.

properties.name: RAF Honington
properties.description:
Support station, home to the vast majority of the RAF Regiment including the RAF Force Protection Headquarters and Force Protection Centre, and numerous RAF Regiment squadrons including No. 20 Wing in the Chemical Biological Radiological and Nuclear role. The station also hosts RAF Police Headquarters and No. 3 RAF Police Wing.

No. 611 Volunteer Gliding Squadron is the only flying unit.

properties.name: RAF Leeming
properties.description:
Home to No. 100 Squadron operating the Hawk T1 for fast jet training and the Northumbrian Universities Air Squadron and No. 11 Air Experience Flight which are both equipped with the Grob Tutor T1.

Non flying units include No. 90 Signals Unit, No. 2 Force Protection Wing, No. 34 Squadron RAF Regiment, No. 609 (West Riding) Auxiliary Squadron and the Joint Forward Air Controllers Training and Standards Unit (JFACTSU).

properties.name: RAF Leuchars
properties.description:
Former RAF Leuchars used to operate Typhoon aircraft, now a small crew of RAF personel maintain the site and continue operation of the ATC tower. The Airfield is a relief landing ground for RAF Lossiemouth.

Continues to operate the Tutor T1 with the East Of Scotland Universities Air Squadron and No. 12 flight.

properties.name: RAF Linton-On-Ouse
properties.description: Training station home to No. 72 Squadron operating the Shorts Tucano T1 as part of No. 1 Flying Training School (1 FTS). This is the first aircraft that pilots streamed for fast jet training will utilise, before moving onto the Hawk T1 then their appropriate OCU. Yorkshire Universities Air Squadron and No. 9 Air Experience Flight operate the Grob Tutor T1 and No. 642 Volunteer Gliding Squadron the Grob Vigilant T1

properties.name: RAF Lossiemouth
properties.description:
Primary QRA site for the north of the UK, operating 3 squadrons of the Eurofighter Typhoon.

Also operates US, and soon, RAF, P8 Posiedons.

properties.name: RAF Marham
properties.description: Primary site of operation for the Tornado GR4 as it is phased out. Undergoing significant works for the arrival of both the RAF and Royal  Navy's F35B aircraft, with RAF no. 617 and NAS 608 respectively.

properties.name: RAF Mona
properties.description: Small site acting as a relief landing ground for Hawk T1s operating out of RAF Valley.

properties.name: RRH Neatishead
properties.description: Remote Radar Head forming part of the UK Air Surveillance and Control System which is managed from RAF Boulmer. The station was formerly known as RAF Neatishead.

properties.name: RAF Northolt
properties.description: Home of No. 32 (The Royal) Squadron operated the BAe 146 and Augusta 109 in the Command Support Air Transport role.

properties.name: RAF Odiham
properties.description: Home of the RAF's Chinook fleet operating under Joint Helicopter Command, comprising No. 18 Squadron, and No. 27 Squadrons. No. 7 Squadron operating various Chinook Mks

properties.name: RRH Portreath
properties.description: Remote Radar Head forming part of the UK Air Surveillance and Control System which is managed from RAF Boulmer. The station was formerly known as RAF Portreath.

properties.name: RAF Scampton
properties.description: Home of the RAF Aerobatic Team (The Red Arrows) flying the BAE Systems Hawk T1A. Non-flying units include No. 1 Air Control Centre and the RAF Mobile Meteorological Unit.

properties.name: RAF Shawbury
properties.description: Home of the tri-service Defence Helicopter Flying School, comprising 60(R) Squadron, No. 660 Squadron Army Air Corps and 705 Naval Air Squadron, flying the Eurocopter Squirrel HT1 and Bell Griffin HT1.

properties.name: MOD St Athan
properties.description: Large maintenance facility, often hosting the Hawk T1. also home to No. 4 School of Technical Training and the University of Wales Air Squadron flying the Grob Tutor T1.

properties.name: RAF St Mawgan
properties.description: Primarily a non-flying station but sees regular use for training purposes. Regular visitors include C17. C130 and A400M.

properties.name: RRH Staxton Wold
properties.description: Remote Radar Head forming part of the UK Air Surveillance and Control System which is managed from RAF Boulmer. The station was formerly known as RAF Staxton Wold.

properties.name: RAF Swanwick
properties.description: Operated by NATS, this is the military element of the large London Area Control Centre, or simply, London Centre. Also provided air traffic control for RAF Northolt.

properties.name: RAF Syverston
properties.description: Home of No. 2 Flying Training School headquarters, the Central Gliding School and No. 644 Volunteer Gliding Squadron.

properties.name: RAF Topcliffe
properties.description: Unmanned Relief Landing Ground for Shorts Tucano T1s operating from RAF Linton-on-Ouse. Home to No. 645 Volunteer Gliding Squadrons flying the Grob Vigilant T1.

properties.name: RRH Trimingham
properties.description: Remote Radar Head, satellite station of RRH Neatishead.

properties.name: RAF Valley
properties.description: Training station home to No. 4 Squadron operating the BAE Systems Hawk T2 and as part of No. 4 Flying Training School. No. 202 Squadron, part of the Defence Helicopter Flying School, operates the Bell Griffin HT2 in the maritime and mountains flying skills training role.The station is also home to the RAF Mountain Rescue Service headquarters, now contracted to civillian operators.

properties.name: RAF Waddington
properties.description:
Main Opearting base for the RAF's Intelligence, Surveillance, Target Acquisition and Reconnaissance (ISTAR) operations. 
This means a wide variety of aircraft are operated;
Boeing Sentry AEW1
Beechcraft Shadow R1
Sentinel R1
RC-135 Rivet Joint.

The future of the RC-135 at Waddington is likely short lived, as the length of the runway greatly hinders operations.

properties.name: RAF Wittering
properties.description:
Support station accommodating elements of the RAF A4 Force which supports deployed and expeditionary air operations. The A4 force comprises a variety of units under No. 42 (Expeditionary Support) Wing and No. 85 (Expeditionary Logistics) Wing.

Flying units consist of No. 16 Squadron of No. 3 Flying Training School and No. 115 Squadron, University of London Air Squadron, Cambridge University Air Squadron and No. 5 Air Experience Flight of No. 6 Flying Training School, all flying the Grob Tutor T1.

properties.name: RAF Woodvale
properties.description: Training station home to Liverpool University Air Squadron, Manchester and Salford Universities Air Squadron, No. 10 Air Experience Flight all flying the Grob Tutor T1and No. 631 Volunteer Gliding Squadron flying the Grob Vigilant T1. The Station is also home to No. 611 (West Lancashire) Squadron RAuxAF.

properties.name: BAE Warton
properties.description: Primary manufacturing site for BAE Systems in the UK, constructing and maintaining Typhoon and Hawk Aircraft. Key parts of the F35 program are also manufactured at a very nearby plant.

properties.name: RAF Wyton
properties.description: Non-flying station operated under Joint Forces Command. Home to the Joint Forces Intelligence Group (JFIG), the Defence Intelligence Fusion Centre (DIFC), No. 42 Engineer Regiment (Geographic) of the British Army.

properties.name: RAF Croughton
properties.description: Non-flying station operated as a signals intelligence facility by the USAF and US intelligence agencies.

properties.name: RAF Fairford
properties.description:
Key site of U2 Flights transitioning between the US and operations in the Middle East.
Also acts as a forward operating base, regularly seeing deployments of US B1, B2 and B52 aircraft.
Backup landing site for RAF Mildenhall.

properties.name: RAF Feltwell
properties.description: Non-flying station, operated as a space intelligence facility by the USAF.

properties.name: RAF Lakenheath
properties.description:
Home to the USAF's 48th Fighter Wing, operating F15-C and E aircraft. Also operates Pavehawk Helicopters.

F35s are expected to base from RAF Lakenheath in the near future, with regular visits from the aircraft starting to take place.

properties.name: RAF Menwith Hill
properties.description: Joint UK/US signals intelligence gathering station which functions primarily as a field station of the US National Security Agency.

properties.name: RAF Mildenhall
properties.description:
Large USAF facility operating multiple types;
KC-135 Stratotanker
RC-135 Rivet Joint
MC-130
CV-22

The station is expected to close in 2023 with the expectation being that US assets at the station (and likely RAF RC-135s) will move to RAF Fairford.

properties.name: RAF Molesworth
properties.description:
Non-flying station, home to the Joint Intelligence Operations Center Europe (JIOCEUR) Analytic Center operated by the US Defense Intelligence Agency and the J2 Directorate which provides US Africa Command with intelligence.

All operations are moving to RAF Croughton.

properties.name: RAF Welford
properties.description: Non-flying station used as ammunition depot in support of bomber operating from RAF Fairford.

properties.name: RAF Akrotiri
properties.description:
Permanent Joint Operating Base acting as a strategic staging airfield to support British military operations in the Middle East. 
Stages combat aircraft for Middle East operations and supports US U2 flights.

Also houses a fleet of Bell Griffin HAR2 helicopters.

properties.name: RAF Ascension
properties.description:
Permanent Joint Operating Base predominately operating as a stopover for flights between RAF Brize Norton in Oxfordshire and RAF Mount Pleasant in the Falkland Islands.
 Also used by the USAF and NASA as a communications and satellite tracking station.

properties.name: RAF Gibraltar
properties.description: Permanent Joint Operating Base acting as a strategic staging airfield. Also functions as Gibraltar International Airport which comprises a civilian passenger terminal operated by Government of Gibraltar.

properties.name: RAF Mount Pleasant
properties.description:
Newly built RAF Station on the Falkland Islands. Houses a permanent force of 4 Typhoon aircraft for Air Defence.

The base sees regular flights to Brize Norton via Ascension of RAF Voyager and Hercules aircraft. 

2 Chinooks are also based at Mount Pleasant for general duties.

properties.name: HMNB Devonport
properties.description:
Largest Naval Base in Western Europe. HMNB Devonport is home to the UK's amphibious Assault capabilities, with both HMS Albion and Bulwark based here.
Also based at Devonport are the UK's fleet of Trafalgar Class Submarines and a number of Type 23 ASW Frigates.

properties.name: RNAS Culdrose
properties.description:
RNAS Culdrose is the main air base of the Royal Navy's air capabilities.
Hawk T1
Merlin Mk2 
Merlin Mk4

properties.name: RNAS Yeovilton
properties.description:
Wildcat
Merlin

properties.name: HMNB Portsmouth
properties.description:
Home to the UK's Aircraft Carrier Fleet. Both HMS Queen Elizabeth and HMS Prince of Wales are to be based here. 

Portsmouth is home to the fleet of 6 Type 45 'Daring Class' destroyers and a number of Type 23 'Duke Class' ASW Frigates.

Also home to a number of Minehunters and OPVs.

properties.name: HMS Excellent
properties.description:
Part of the maritime warfare school and home to the Navy Command Headquarters.
Also home to static training ship HMS Bristol

properties.name: RNAS Merryfield
properties.description: Part of the Fleet Air Arm and primarily used for helicopter training and as a backup for RNAS Yeovilton.

properties.name: HMNB Clyde/Faslane
properties.description:
Faslane is the primary home of the UK's submarine force, with both the Vanguard Class Ballistic Missile Submarines and the Astute Class being based here. By 2020, all UK submarines will be based at Faslane.
She see's regular visits from US submarines such as the Los Angeles Class.

Faslane is also home to a fleet of OPVs for fishery protection.

properties.name: HMS Collingwood
properties.description: HMS Collingwood is the largest naval training organisation in Europe, home to the Maritime Warfare School.

properties.name: HMS Dartmouth
properties.description: Home of the Royal Naval College with the Hindostan moored as a static training ship.

properties.name: HMS Raleigh
properties.description: This large facility is the primary basic training site for the Royal Navy. It's facilities include damage control and fire fighting trainers, additionally to this, former HMS brecon is moored as a static training ship.

properties.name: HMS Sultan
properties.description: Primary training facility for the Royal Navy's engineers.

properties.name: Rosyth Dockyard
properties.description: Key construction and maintenance facility for the Royal Navy and BAE Systems. Both Queen Elizabeth Class Carriers assembled at Rosyth and a number of decomissioned submarines are moored here.

Possible part of Area 51

properties.name: Air Force Research Laboratory
properties.description: A scientific research organization operated by the United States Air Force Materiel Command dedicated to leading the discovery, development, and integration of affordable aerospace warfighting technologies, planning and executing the Air Force science and technology program, and provide warfighting capabilities to United States air, space, and cyberspace forces.
